052-dc-aware-adc3bit.title: DC Aware ADC3bit
052-dc-aware-adc3bit.syntax_focus: Use static voltage-domain quantization so the ADC output is valid without a sampling clock.
052-dc-aware-adc3bit.description: Combinational three-bit ADC with clipping, binary output ordering, and DC-compatible static behavior.
required_behavior: Clip the analog input to the 0-to-vref range.
Quantize the clipped input into eight uniform bin-floor codes.
Drive d2..d0 as MSB-to-LSB voltage-coded outputs without requiring a clock.
Use smooth output transitions while preserving static conversion semantics.
visible_tests: visible.scs sweeps static input levels across low, middle, high, and over-range ADC bins.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs uses different static levels including negative and over-full-scale clipping cases.

053-latched-bus-dac8.title: Latched Bus DAC8
053-latched-bus-dac8.syntax_focus: Use rising clock events to latch a voltage-coded input bus and hold the DAC output between updates.
053-latched-bus-dac8.description: Eight-bit update-strobed DAC that samples a parallel bus on clock edges and holds the decoded output.
required_behavior: Sample b7..b0 on rising vclk crossings with b7 as MSB.
Hold the last latched code between update edges even if the input bus changes.
Map code zero to 0 V and code 255 to vref.
Smooth output transitions after each clocked update.
visible_tests: visible.scs changes the input bus between update-clock edges to exercise latch-and-hold behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs uses different bus codes and between-edge bus changes to check rising-edge sampling and hold behavior.

054-thermometer-bus-encoder.title: Thermometer Bus Encoder
054-thermometer-bus-encoder.syntax_focus: Use voltage-domain quantization and multi-output thermometer bus driving.
054-thermometer-bus-encoder.description: Analog-input 16-segment thermometer bus encoder with prefix output ordering.
required_behavior: Clip the analog input to the declared full-scale range.
Turn on a monotonic prefix of t0..t15 according to the input level.
Avoid binary-coded output words and reversed segment ordering.
visible_tests: visible.scs covers low, midscale, high, and overrange input levels.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs covers underrange, alternate midscale, and overrange levels.

055-slew-rate-dac4.title: Slew Rate DAC4
055-slew-rate-dac4.syntax_focus: Use slew() to implement a finite-slew voltage-domain DAC output.
055-slew-rate-dac4.description: Four-bit voltage-coded DAC with Cadence-style slew-rate-limited analog output.
required_behavior: Decode d3..d0 as an unsigned binary word with d3 as MSB.
Map code zero to 0 V and code fifteen to vref.
Limit output motion with the public slew rate rather than jumping directly.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises large up/down code steps and settled endpoint behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs uses different code transitions to check both slew and bit order.

056-correlated-double-sampler.title: Correlated Double Sampler
056-correlated-double-sampler.syntax_focus: Use separate reset and signal sampling events to form a held signal-minus-reset correction.
056-correlated-double-sampler.description: Two-phase correlated double sampler for a data-converter front end with held corrected output and valid state.
required_behavior: Sample the reset level on rising phi_reset and clear valid.
Sample the signal level on rising phi_signal and output the clamped signal-minus-reset correction around vcm.
Assert valid only after a completed signal sample and return vout to vcm on reset reacquisition.
Hold vout and valid between sampling events.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises positive, negative, and high-clipped reset/signal sample pairs with held outputs.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes sampling phase timing and input sequence, including reset reacquisition and low/high clipping coverage.

057-first-order-sigma-delta-modulator.title: First Order Sigma Delta Modulator
057-first-order-sigma-delta-modulator.syntax_focus: Use clocked analog events and state update to model a first-order sigma-delta loop.
057-first-order-sigma-delta-modulator.description: Clocked first-order sigma-delta modulator with one-bit voltage-coded output stream.
required_behavior: Update a feedback-bounded accumulator on rising clock crossings.
Subtract the previous one-bit feedback value from the normalized input.
Emit a one-bit stream whose pulse density rises with the input level.
visible_tests: visible.scs checks low and high input-density windows.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies clock period and input levels to catch hard-coded sequences.

075-adc-static-linearity-monitor.title: ADC Static Linearity Monitor
075-adc-static-linearity-monitor.syntax_focus: Use voltage-domain decoding and sampled retained state to measure maximum static ADC code error.
075-adc-static-linearity-monitor.description: Sampled measurement monitor that tracks the maximum code error between a swept input and observed three-bit ADC output.
required_behavior: Update only on rising vsample crossings.
Compute the ideal bin-floor three-bit code from the clipped analog input.
Decode observed d2..d0 with d2 as MSB.
Compute absolute code error in LSBs and retain the maximum sampled error seen so far.
Drive maxerr as the retained maximum error scaled by lsb_out.
visible_tests: visible.scs strobes settled ADC sweep points and includes recoveries after code-error intervals.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs uses different sampled sweep points to check one-LSB and two-LSB maximum-error retention.

301-function-clamp-window.title: Function Clamp Window
301-function-clamp-window.syntax_focus: Use a user-defined function to clamp an input window.
301-function-clamp-window.description: Use a user-defined function to clamp an input window. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Function Clamp Window.
required_behavior: Use a Verilog-A user-defined function named clamp_window or an equivalently clear function to compute the sampled value.
On each rising crossing of clk through vth = 0.45 V, sample V(vin) and update.
Drive both outputs with transition(..., 0, tr, tr) using tr = 200p.
out = 0.1 when vin < 0.1.
out = vin when 0.1 <= vin <= 0.8.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Function Clamp Window stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Function Clamp Window stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

304-function-code-normalizer.syntax_focus: Use a user-defined function for code normalization.

305-function-soft-threshold.syntax_focus: Use a user-defined function for a soft threshold curve.

306-case-mode-gain-selector.title: Case Mode Gain Selector
306-case-mode-gain-selector.syntax_focus: Use a case statement to select voltage-domain behavior.
306-case-mode-gain-selector.description: Use a case statement to select voltage-domain behavior. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Case Mode Gain Selector.
required_behavior: Use a Verilog-A case statement to select one of four clocked gain modes.
On each rising crossing of clk through vth = 0.45 V, sample V(mode) and convert it to an integer state.
Then use case (state_q) to update.
When rst crosses high or is high during a clock event, reset state and both outputs to zero.
state 0 when mode < 0.225.

307-case-priority-status-decoder.title: Case Priority Status Decoder
307-case-priority-status-decoder.syntax_focus: Use a case statement to select voltage-domain behavior.
307-case-priority-status-decoder.description: Use a case statement to select voltage-domain behavior. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Case Priority Status Decoder.
required_behavior: Use a Verilog-A case statement to decode a priority status code.
On each rising crossing of clk through vth = 0.45 V, compute state_q with this priority order.
Use case (state_q) to drive out and metric to 0.00, 0.30, 0.60, or 0.90 V for states 0, 1, 2, and 3.
if vin > 0.75, state 3.
else if mode > 0.60, state 2.

On each rising crossing of clk through vth = 0.45 V, compute.

state 0 when vin < 0.225.
state 1 when 0.225 <= vin < 0.45.

bucket/state 0 when vin < 0.30.

310-case-resettable-state-decoder.title: Case Resettable State Decoder
310-case-resettable-state-decoder.syntax_focus: Use a case statement to select voltage-domain behavior.
310-case-resettable-state-decoder.description: Use a case statement to select voltage-domain behavior. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Case Resettable State Decoder.
required_behavior: Use a Verilog-A case statement to decode a resettable clocked state machine.
The model keeps an integer state_q initialized to zero.
Use case (state_q) to drive both out and metric to 0.00, 0.30, 0.60, or 0.90 V for states 0, 1, 2, and 3.
if rst is high, reset state_q, out, and metric to zero.
otherwise, when mode > 0.45, advance state_q by one, wrapping from 3 back to 0.

hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Case Resettable State Decoder stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

311-for-loop-running-average.title: For Loop Running Average
311-for-loop-running-average.syntax_focus: Use a for loop and array state for voltage-domain computation.
311-for-loop-running-average.description: Use a for loop and array state for voltage-domain computation. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for For Loop Running Average.
required_behavior: Use a Verilog-A for loop and a four-entry real array to implement a clocked running average.
The model keeps samples[0:3], initialized to zero.
A rising rst event must also clear the array immediately.
if rst is high, clear all samples, clear the valid sample count, and drive both outputs to zero.
otherwise shift the history toward older indexes, store the new V(vin) in samples[0], and increment the valid sample count up to four.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public For Loop Running Average stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the For Loop Running Average stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

312-for-loop-thermometer-count.title: For Loop Thermometer Count
312-for-loop-thermometer-count.syntax_focus: Use a for loop and array state for voltage-domain computation.
312-for-loop-thermometer-count.description: Use a for loop and array state for voltage-domain computation. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for For Loop Thermometer Count.
required_behavior: Use a Verilog-A for loop over a four-entry real array to count how many thermometer thresholds the sampled input exceeds.
The model defines thresholds[0:3] = {0.18, 0.36, 0.54, 0.72}.
A rising rst event must also clear the count immediately.
if rst is high, clear the count and drive both outputs to zero.
otherwise set count_q = 0.

hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the For Loop Thermometer Count stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

if rst is high, clear all samples and drive both outputs to zero.
otherwise shift the history toward older indexes and store the new V(vin) in samples[0].

if rst is high, clear all samples and drive both outputs to zero.

hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the For Loop Windowed Peak stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

otherwise derive four bits.

Drive both out and metric with the queried value divided by 300.0.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Simparam Query Tnom stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Simparam Query Tnom stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

468-branch-declaration-voltage-probe.title: Branch Declaration Voltage Probe
468-branch-declaration-voltage-probe.syntax_focus: Declare an explicit branch and read its voltage.
468-branch-declaration-voltage-probe.description: Declare a named branch between p and n, then mirror that branch voltage to out.
required_behavior: Drive out to V(p,n) through V(br), not to the absolute p potential.
Track changes in p while n is biased at a non-zero reference.
Use only voltage-domain behavior and no current contribution.
visible_tests: visible.scs sweeps p with n fixed at 0.10 V and checks V(p,n).
hidden_tests: hidden.scs adds an extra low-voltage segment to catch absolute-node probes.

469-current-contribution-conductance.title: Current Contribution Conductance
469-current-contribution-conductance.syntax_focus: Use I(p,n) current contribution for a conductance-style model.
469-current-contribution-conductance.description: Use I(p,n) <+ gain*V(p,n) as a conductance-style conservative current contribution and expose the probed branch current on imon.
required_behavior: Contribute branch current proportional to V(p,n).
Expose the contributed branch current through the DUT imon monitor port.
Keep the conservative current contribution as the modeled circuit behavior.
visible_tests: visible.scs biases p/n and records imon from the DUT branch-current monitor.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes p and n independently, including a negative branch voltage, to catch absolute-node, positive-only, wrong-sign, and wrong-scale current contributions.

470-branch-current-probe-contribution.title: Branch Current Probe Contribution
470-branch-current-probe-contribution.syntax_focus: Use a named branch with current contribution and current probing.
470-branch-current-probe-contribution.description: Use a named branch with current contribution and current probing to mirror branch current to out.
required_behavior: Contribute current through the named branch.
Probe the branch current and drive out from the measured current value.
Remain staged until branch-current probing and KCL behavior are certified.
visible_tests: visible.scs drives bias points that should produce measurable branch current.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes current magnitude to catch zero or wrong-scale current probes.

471-indirect-branch-null-balance.title: Indirect Branch Null Balance
471-indirect-branch-null-balance.syntax_focus: Use an indirect branch assignment target/equation form.
471-indirect-branch-null-balance.description: Use an indirect branch assignment equation to impose a null-balance relationship between out and in.
required_behavior: Declare the indirect branch equation form.
Constrain out with V(out) : V(out) == V(in).
Drive the observable output from the solved branch relationship across the visible and hidden PWL segments.
visible_tests: visible.scs checks that out tracks the driven input through the first PWL sequence.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs adds a late input segment to reject disconnected, offset, and scaled equations.

472-indirect-branch-ddt-balance.title: Indirect Branch Ddt Balance
472-indirect-branch-ddt-balance.syntax_focus: Use indirect branch assignment with a ddt() equation term.
472-indirect-branch-ddt-balance.description: Use indirect branch assignment with ddt() to express a stable first-order dynamic branch-balance equation.
required_behavior: Combine indirect branch equation solving with a ddt() dynamic term.
Declare tau = 20n from (0:inf).
Solve ddt(V(out)) == (V(in)-V(out))/tau through the indirect branch equation.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises a dynamic input and checks the resulting first-order trajectory.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs adds a late input segment to catch zero, biased, wrong-scale, or wrong-time-constant dynamics.

473-attribute-potential-abstol-probe.title: Attribute Potential Abstol Probe
473-attribute-potential-abstol-probe.syntax_focus: Access a node potential attribute through hierarchical attribute syntax.
473-attribute-potential-abstol-probe.description: Access a node potential attribute through hierarchical attribute syntax. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Attribute Potential Abstol Probe.
required_behavior: Read the input node potential absolute tolerance with in.potential.abstol.
Store the attribute value in a real variable.
Drive out as V(in) + 1e5 * in.potential.abstol.
Smooth out with transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
Use only voltage-domain contributions; do not use I(...).
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Attribute Potential Abstol Probe stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Attribute Potential Abstol Probe stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

474-generic-potential-access-function.title: Generic Potential Access Function
474-generic-potential-access-function.syntax_focus: Use the generic potential() access function for an electrical node.
474-generic-potential-access-function.description: Use the generic potential() access function for an electrical node. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Generic Potential Access Function.
required_behavior: Read the input node potential using the generic access function potential(in).
Drive out with the value returned by potential(in).
Smooth out with transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
Use only voltage-domain contributions; do not use I(...).
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Generic Potential Access Function stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Generic Potential Access Function stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

475-generic-potential-contribution.title: Generic Potential Contribution
475-generic-potential-contribution.syntax_focus: Use the generic potential() access function as a contribution target.
475-generic-potential-contribution.description: Use the generic potential() access function as a contribution target. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Generic Potential Contribution.
required_behavior: Drive the output through the generic contribution target potential(out) <+ ....
The driven output value must track the input voltage V(in).
Smooth the contribution with transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
Use only voltage-domain behavior; do not use I(...).
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Generic Potential Contribution stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Generic Potential Contribution stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

476-oomr-string-voltage-probe.title: Oomr String Voltage Probe
476-oomr-string-voltage-probe.syntax_focus: Use a string out-of-module reference in a voltage probe.
476-oomr-string-voltage-probe.description: Use a string out-of-module reference parameter to probe the top-level vin voltage.
required_behavior: Resolve the sigpath string to the top-level vin node.
Drive out to the referenced vin waveform, including positive and negative levels.
Do not hard-code a constant or local dummy node.
visible_tests: visible.scs drives vin through two positive levels and checks out follows vin.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs adds a negative vin segment to reject clamp-only or constant implementations.

477-analog-node-alias-initial.title: Analog Node Alias Initial
477-analog-node-alias-initial.syntax_focus: Use $analog_node_alias inside analog initial for hierarchical node aliasing.
477-analog-node-alias-initial.description: Create an alias to a top-level vin node during analog initial and mirror the aliased node voltage.
required_behavior: $analog_node_alias must bind the internal aliased node to the configured target path.
out must follow the aliased vin waveform across all visible and hidden segments.
The alias setup must happen in analog initial rather than by directly probing vin.
visible_tests: visible.scs checks alias tracking for two positive vin levels.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs adds a negative vin segment to catch unbound or constant aliases.

478-inherited-port-attribute-supply.title: Inherited Port Attribute Supply
478-inherited-port-attribute-supply.syntax_focus: Declare inherited terminal attributes on supply-like ports.
478-inherited-port-attribute-supply.description: Declare inherited terminal attributes on supply-like ports. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Inherited Port Attribute Supply.
required_behavior: Declare out as output, in as input, and vdd/gnd as inout supply-like ports.
Attach inherited connection attributes to vdd and gnd using inh_conn_prop_name and inh_conn_def_value.
Drive V(out, gnd) from V(in, gnd) using transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
Use the inherited-port attribute syntax even when the testbench connects the supply ports explicitly.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Inherited Port Attribute Supply stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Inherited Port Attribute Supply stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

479-inherited-mfactor-parameter.title: Inherited Mfactor Parameter
479-inherited-mfactor-parameter.syntax_focus: Declare an inherited m-factor parameter attribute.
479-inherited-mfactor-parameter.description: Declare an inherited m-factor parameter attribute. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Inherited Mfactor Parameter.
required_behavior: Declare a real parameter named m with the Verilog-A attribute (* inherited_mfactor *).
Drive out with m * V(in) using transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
The testbench may override m; your model must use the parameter value, not a hard-coded gain.
Use only voltage-domain behavior; do not use I(...).
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Inherited Mfactor Parameter stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Inherited Mfactor Parameter stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

480-mfactor-system-function-gain.title: Mfactor System Function Gain
480-mfactor-system-function-gain.description: Read the instance m factor through Cadence-compatible $mfactor syntax and use it as a behavioral gain.
required_behavior: For an instance with m=2.0, drive out to two times the input voltage.
Track positive and negative input levels without using a hard-coded waveform.
Compile and run without current-domain contributions.
visible_tests: visible.scs instantiates the DUT with m=2.0 and drives positive input levels.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs adds a negative input segment to check signed gain behavior.

481-analog-primitive-resistor-instance.title: Analog Primitive Resistor Instance
481-analog-primitive-resistor-instance.syntax_focus: Instantiate a Spectre analog resistor primitive inside a Verilog-A module.
481-analog-primitive-resistor-instance.description: Instantiate a Spectre resistor primitive inside the Verilog-A module as a structure-certified language-extension artifact.
required_behavior: Instantiate exactly one resistor primitive as an internal analog primitive.
Use .r(1000.0) and positional ports (p, n).
Certify primitive-instantiation structure only; resistor current and full MNA/KCL behavior are intentionally outside this row.
visible_tests: visible.scs provides a bias smoke deck while the artifact checker verifies the primitive structure.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes the bias condition while the artifact checker catches missing or wrong primitive instances.

482-analog-primitive-isource-instance.title: Analog Primitive Isource Instance
482-analog-primitive-isource-instance.syntax_focus: Instantiate a Spectre analog current-source primitive inside a Verilog-A module.
482-analog-primitive-isource-instance.description: Instantiate a Spectre current-source primitive inside the Verilog-A module as a structure-certified language-extension artifact.
required_behavior: Declare parameter real ibias = 1u.
Instantiate exactly one current-source primitive using .dc(ibias) and positional ports (n, p).
Certify primitive-instantiation structure only; current-source circuit behavior and full MNA/KCL behavior are intentionally outside this row.
visible_tests: visible.scs provides a bias smoke deck while the artifact checker verifies the primitive structure.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the external bias while the artifact checker catches missing or wrong primitive source instances.

483-cds-violation-threshold-assert.title: Cds Violation Threshold Assert
483-cds-violation-threshold-assert.syntax_focus: Use $cds_violation() to emit a Verilog-A assert violation.
483-cds-violation-threshold-assert.description: Use $cds_violation() to emit a Verilog-A assert violation. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Cds Violation Threshold Assert.
required_behavior: Monitor V(in) continuously.
When V(in) > 1.0, call $cds_violation("va_threshold", "threshold_exceeded", V(in)).
Drive out with V(in) while V(in) <= 1.0.
Drive out with 1.0 while V(in) > 1.0, using the same threshold condition as the violation call.
Smooth out with transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Cds Violation Threshold Assert stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Cds Violation Threshold Assert stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

484-rtoi-conversion-quantizer.title: Rtoi Conversion Quantizer
484-rtoi-conversion-quantizer.syntax_focus: Use $rtoi() to convert real-valued code to integer state.
484-rtoi-conversion-quantizer.description: Use $rtoi() to convert real-valued code to integer state. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Rtoi Conversion Quantizer.
required_behavior: Compute code = $rtoi(8.0 * V(in)).
Saturate code to the integer range [0, 7].
Drive out with code / 7.0 using transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
Use $rtoi() for the real-to-integer conversion; do not hand-roll the conversion with threshold-only logic.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Rtoi Conversion Quantizer stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Rtoi Conversion Quantizer stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

485-mc-trial-number-metric.title: Mc Trial Number Metric
485-mc-trial-number-metric.syntax_focus: Use $cds_get_mc_trial_number() to expose the Monte Carlo trial index.
485-mc-trial-number-metric.description: Use $cds_get_mc_trial_number() to expose the Monte Carlo trial index. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Mc Trial Number Metric.
required_behavior: In an @(initial_step) block, read the trial index with $cds_get_mc_trial_number().
Store the result in an integer named trial_num.
Drive out with 0.25 + 0.1 * trial_num using transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
In the default non-Monte-Carlo transient tests, trial_num is expected to be 0, so out should settle to 0.25.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Mc Trial Number Metric stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Mc Trial Number Metric stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

486-rf-source-info-registration.title: Rf Source Info Registration
486-rf-source-info-registration.syntax_focus: Use $cds_set_rf_source_info() to register an RF source name and frequency.
486-rf-source-info-registration.description: Use $cds_set_rf_source_info() to register an RF source name and frequency. This task is verified with executable visible and hidden benches for Rf Source Info Registration.
required_behavior: Declare parameter string fundname = "rf".
Declare parameter real freq = 1G.
In the continuous analog context, call $cds_set_rf_source_info(fundname, freq).
Drive out with V(in) + freq / 1.0e10 using transition(..., 0, 200p, 200p).
The output term makes the registered frequency parameter observable in ordinary transient tests.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Rf Source Info Registration stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Rf Source Info Registration stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

487-table-model-2d-array-surface.title: Table Model 2d Array Surface
487-table-model-2d-array-surface.syntax_focus: Use two independent arrays and one output array in a 2D $table_model().
487-table-model-2d-array-surface.description: Use array-backed 2D $table_model data to map x/y voltage corners to a surface output.
required_behavior: Initialize x, y, and z arrays at initial_step.
$table_model(V(xnode), V(ynode), x, y, z, "1L,1L") must return the expected corner value.
out must distinguish all exercised 2D corners rather than returning a 1D or constant result.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the (0,0), (1,0), and (1,1) corners.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs also exercises the (0,1) corner to catch collapsed-axis implementations.

488-table-model-string-param-source.title: Table Model String Param Source
488-table-model-string-param-source.syntax_focus: Use a string parameter as the $table_model() data source.
488-table-model-string-param-source.description: Load 1D $table_model data from the string parameter tmdata and mirror the interpolated gain profile.
required_behavior: Read the gain_profile.tbl support artifact through the tmdata string parameter.
Return 0.1, 0.6, and 1.1 at the exercised input breakpoints 0.0, 0.5, and 1.0.
The behavior must depend on the table file contents rather than hard-coded input thresholds.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises all three table breakpoints in ascending order.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs replays the first breakpoint after the high endpoint to catch stateful replay bugs.

489-event-nested-or-expression.title: Event Nested Or Expression
489-event-nested-or-expression.syntax_focus: Use a deeper analog event expression with nested cross/above/timer terms.
489-event-nested-or-expression.description: Use one nested analog event expression combining cross(), above(), and timer() terms with or. This is a language-extension/L0 support row outside the original full-300 circuit-function claim.
required_behavior: Initialize real state q to 0.0 at initial_step.
Use one analog event statement combining cross(V(a) - 0.45, +1), above(V(b) - 0.45), and timer(10n, 20n) with or.
On every event, increment q by 0.1.
Wrap q back to 0.0 when q > 0.9.
Drive out with q using transition smoothing.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises the public Event Nested Or Expression stimulus and checks the primary observable behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the Event Nested Or Expression stimulus to catch hard-coded, threshold-shifted, reset-biased, or metric-only implementations.

491-kcl-capacitor-ddt-current.title: Kcl Capacitor Ddt Current
491-kcl-capacitor-ddt-current.syntax_focus: Use current contribution with ddt() for a capacitor-style conservative model.
491-kcl-capacitor-ddt-current.description: Use I(p,n) <+ C*ddt(V(p,n)) to model a capacitor-style conservative current contribution and expose the probed dynamic branch current on imon.
required_behavior: Contribute current proportional to the time derivative of branch voltage.
Expose the dynamic branch-current contribution through the DUT imon monitor port.
Keep the conservative ddt current contribution as the modeled circuit behavior.
visible_tests: visible.scs applies rising and falling V(p,n) ramps and records imon from the DUT branch-current monitor.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes both p and n slopes to catch static, p-only, wrong-sign, and wrong-scale ddt current substitutes.

492-kcl-inductor-idt-voltage.title: Kcl Inductor Idt Voltage
492-kcl-inductor-idt-voltage.syntax_focus: Use voltage contribution with idt() of branch current for an inductor-style model.
492-kcl-inductor-idt-voltage.description: Use voltage contribution with idt() of branch current to model an inductor-style conservative element with Spectre branch-current polarity.
required_behavior: Integrate branch current to determine the contributed branch voltage.
Participate in the KCL/MNA solution with internal dynamic state.
Match Spectre branch-current sign convention for independent current sources.
visible_tests: visible.scs drives a current-related condition to expose integrated voltage behavior.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes stimulus duration to catch missing integration state.

493-continuous-laplace-nd-filter.title: Continuous Laplace Nd Filter
493-continuous-laplace-nd-filter.syntax_focus: Use laplace_nd() as a continuous-time behavioral transfer function.
493-continuous-laplace-nd-filter.description: Use laplace_nd() as a continuous-time behavioral transfer function with visible dynamic output.
required_behavior: Apply the numerator/denominator transfer function to the input waveform.
Produce a certified dynamic response rather than direct input feedthrough.
Remain staged until continuous laplace_nd solver accuracy is certified.
visible_tests: visible.scs drives a transition to check continuous transfer response.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs varies the transition profile to catch hard-coded or static outputs.

494-continuous-zi-nd-filter.title: Continuous Zi Nd Filter
494-continuous-zi-nd-filter.syntax_focus: Use zi_nd() as a sampled-data behavioral transfer function.
494-continuous-zi-nd-filter.description: Use zi_nd() as a sampled-data behavioral transfer function with visible stateful output.
required_behavior: Apply sampled-data numerator/denominator filtering to the input waveform.
Preserve filter state across sample intervals.
Match Spectre sampled-data zi_nd response at the configured sample interval.
visible_tests: visible.scs drives a sequence that should update zi_nd filter state.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs uses alternate input timing to catch missing sampled-state behavior.

495-supply-bias-validity-gate.title: Supply Bias Validity Gate
495-supply-bias-validity-gate.syntax_focus: Combine supply window, local-ground displacement, vss-referenced bias validity, enable, and power-down into voltage-coded validity outputs.
495-supply-bias-validity-gate.description: Reusable voltage-domain gate that reports valid analog supply/bias conditions and downstream drive enable.
required_behavior: Measure supply as V(vdd,vss) and require it to fall inside the public min/max supply window.
Reject operation when the local vss rail is displaced beyond the public ground limit.
Measure bias as V(vbias,vss) and require it to fall inside the public bias window.
Drive ok high only for valid supply, ground, and bias conditions.
Drive gated high only when ok is high, en is high, and pd is low.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises nominal operation, undervoltage, high-supply fault, bias fault, ground displacement, vss-referenced bias under a legal local-ground offset, and power-down gating.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes the supply, vss offset, bias, enable, and power-down sequence to check vss-referenced bias behavior.

496-reference-settling-window-monitor.title: Reference Settling Window Monitor
496-reference-settling-window-monitor.syntax_focus: Sample absolute reference error against a target, track consecutive in-window settling, and expose a bounded error metric.
496-reference-settling-window-monitor.description: Measurement-style monitor for deciding when a reference or bias voltage has remained settled inside a target window.
required_behavior: Sample absolute error between ref and target on rising clock crossings.
Drive err_metric as clipped absolute error scaled by the public full-scale error.
Clear settling state while active-high reset is asserted.
Accumulate consecutive in-window samples and clear the accumulator on any out-of-window sample.
Assert valid only after the public settling count and drive settle_mon as bounded voltage-coded progress.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises reset, target changes, out-of-window intervals, valid assertion, and valid clearing.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes clock phase, reset timing, target levels, and settling/out-of-window intervals.

497-power-enable-turnon-delay-gate.title: Power Enable Turn-On Delay Gate
497-power-enable-turnon-delay-gate.syntax_focus: Require consecutive valid sampled supply, bias, enable, and power-down conditions before releasing downstream drive.
497-power-enable-turnon-delay-gate.description: Power-up sequencing gate that exposes instantaneous validity and delayed drive-enable release for a biased analog block.
required_behavior: Sample supply, bias, enable, and power-down conditions on rising clock crossings.
Measure supply as V(vdd,vss) and bias as V(vbias,vss).
Drive pwr_ok high only for sampled valid supply, bias, enable, and non-power-down state.
Accumulate consecutive valid sampled updates and clear the accumulator on any sampled invalid condition.
Assert drive_en only after the public turn-on delay count and drive delay_mon as bounded voltage-coded progress.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises nominal release, supply undervoltage, bias fault, disabled interval, and power-down interval.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes clock phase, local vss offset, high-supply fault, low-bias fault, enable drop, and power-down interval.

498-power-mode-supply-current-metric.title: Power Mode Supply Current Metric
498-power-mode-supply-current-metric.syntax_focus: Expose supply-current demand as a voltage-coded metric that scales with supply, mode, load, enable, and power-down state.
498-power-mode-supply-current-metric.description: Voltage-domain macro-model metric for supply-current accounting across operating mode, load demand, and power state.
required_behavior: Scale the metric by V(vdd,vss)/vnom with clipping.
Use the power-down metric whenever en is low or pd is high.
Use a mode-dependent active quiescent metric while enabled.
Add a clipped load-dependent term during active operation.
Drive isup_metric with smooth voltage-domain behavior only.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises active low-current and high-current modes, supply scaling, load changes, disable, and power-down.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs uses different rail, mode, load, enable, and power-down sequences including a vss offset.

499-dynamic-supply-level-driver.title: Dynamic Supply Level Driver
499-dynamic-supply-level-driver.syntax_focus: Use local supply rails for both input thresholding and output level generation.
499-dynamic-supply-level-driver.description: Electrical level driver whose input decision and output levels track the local vdd/vss rails and fall back under low supply.
required_behavior: Normalize din relative to the local V(vdd,vss) range above vss.
Drive the output low or high relative to the local rails while the supply is valid.
Drive the local low level when V(vdd,vss) is below vsup_min.
Handle nonzero vss offsets correctly.
Smooth output transitions with transition().
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises fixed and shifted local rails, valid and invalid supply intervals, local-threshold input decisions under a shifted vss rail, and low/high output levels.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes rail amplitudes, vss offset, input levels, and under-supply intervals.

500-rail-ramp-rate-startup-monitor.title: Rail Ramp Rate Startup Monitor
500-rail-ramp-rate-startup-monitor.syntax_focus: Sample local rail movement, qualify startup ramp rate, and delay readiness until the rail has settled.
500-rail-ramp-rate-startup-monitor.description: Startup monitor for a locally supplied analog block that checks supply window, ramp rate, settling, and readiness.
required_behavior: Sample V(vdd,vss) on rising clock crossings and compute movement from the previous sampled rail value.
Drive rail_ok high only when en is high and the sampled local rail is inside the public operating window.
Drive ramp_ok high during startup only when sampled positive rail movement is inside the public ramp-rate window.
Above the ready threshold, require sampled movement to stay inside the public settling bound before counting readiness.
Assert startup_ready only after the public number of consecutive settled sampled updates and report bounded sampled slew on slew_metric.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises ramping below ready, delayed settled readiness, high-rail fault, enable clearing, vss offset, and large downward rail movement.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes clock phase, ramp increments, vss offset polarity, high-supply fault timing, and enable clearing.

501-differential-common-mode-window-moni.title: Differential Common Mode Window Monitor
501-differential-common-mode-window-moni.syntax_focus: Compute differential magnitude and common-mode error for an enabled analog-front-end input-validity monitor.
501-differential-common-mode-window-moni.description: Differential input monitor that validates linear-range differential amplitude and common-mode proximity to a reference.
required_behavior: Continuously compute vdiff as V(vip)-V(vin) and vcm as the average of vip and vin.
Drive diff_ok high only while enabled and abs(vdiff) is inside the public differential window.
Drive cm_ok high only while enabled and abs(vcm-V(vcm_ref)) is inside the public common-mode window.
Drive valid high only when both differential and common-mode checks pass under enable.
Expose clipped voltage-coded diff_metric and cm_metric values using the public full-scale parameters.
visible_tests: visible.scs exercises valid input, differential overrange, common-mode overrange, vcm_ref change, enable clearing, and metric scaling.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs changes reference level, input polarity, overrange cases, and enable timing.

502-sine-vco-idtmod-bound-step.title: Sine VCO With Idtmod And Bound Step
502-sine-vco-idtmod-bound-step.syntax_focus: Use idtmod() phase integration and $bound_step() timestep control in a voltage-domain sine VCO.
502-sine-vco-idtmod-bound-step.description: Continuous-time sine VCO whose control voltage sets positive instantaneous frequency, wrapped phase, bipolar sine output, and phase metric.
required_behavior: Integrate center_freq + vco_gain*V(vin) with idtmod() as a modulo-1 phase.
Drive a bipolar sine output centered at 0 V and a wrapped-phase metric scaled by vco_amp.
Call $bound_step from the public points-per-cycle parameter when the operating frequency is positive.
visible_tests: visible.scs holds vin at one constant operating point and checks the sine/phase relationship.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs uses a different constant vin so frequency control and phase reintegration are checked away from the visible point.

503-differential-vco-clip-idtmod.title: Differential VCO With Clip And Idtmod
503-differential-vco-clip-idtmod.syntax_focus: Use a public clip helper and idtmod() phase integration for a fully differential sine VCO.
503-differential-vco-clip-idtmod.description: Fully differential sine VCO with clipped instantaneous frequency, opposite output arms around common mode, and wrapped-phase metric.
required_behavior: Compute frequency from Fnom + dFdV*V(vinp,vinm) and clamp it into [Fmin,Fmax].
Integrate the clipped frequency with idtmod() as a modulo-1 phase.
Drive outp/outm as opposite sine arms around Vcm and metric as the wrapped phase.
visible_tests: visible.scs checks an in-band differential-control operating point.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs drives a raw frequency above Fmax so the clamp behavior is exercised.

504-charge-pump-pfd-state-machine.title: Charge Pump PFD State Machine
504-charge-pump-pfd-state-machine.syntax_focus: Use @cross edge events, @timer sampled integration, and transition-smoothed voltage outputs in a tri-state PFD.
504-charge-pump-pfd-state-machine.description: Voltage-domain charge-pump PFD model whose reference/feedback edge order drives a tri-state detector, bounded vctrl, and state metric.
required_behavior: Increment/decrement a clamped -1/0/+1 detector state on reference and feedback rising crossings.
Update a bounded control-voltage monitor on timer ticks from the detector state.
Move vctrl toward the rail implied by the lead/lag relation and report detector polarity on metric.
visible_tests: visible.scs drives feedback leading reference and expects vctrl to move downward.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs drives reference leading feedback and expects vctrl to move upward.

505-fractional-n-divider-accumulator-flo.title: Fractional-N Divider Accumulator Flow
505-fractional-n-divider-accumulator-flo.syntax_focus: Use a fractional accumulator divider, DCO timer scheduling, PFD-style tracking, and reacquire lock behavior.
505-fractional-n-divider-accumulator-flo.description: Fractional-N PLL flow with dithered feedback division, bounded control monitor, lock drop, and reference-step reacquisition.
required_behavior: Generate a DCO clock and a feedback clock whose toggle counts are dithered by a modulo accumulator.
Use proportional plus bounded-integral phase correction to move vctrl_mon within the supply rails.
Drop lock around a reference-period step and reassert lock after reacquisition.
visible_tests: visible.scs uses one fractional word and a small reference-period step for smoke reacquisition.
hidden_tests: hidden.scs uses a different fractional word and step to check reacquisition and fractional divide behavior.
